title: Asuka Langley Soryu
wikitext:
[]: Asuka Langley Soryu             Quote Post by Animejunkie » Sun Oct 10, 2010 5:45 am I've done a little research on topic. Most of my info on topic is straight from the wiki. Feel free to correct me on any incorrectness in this post. To the best of my understanding Asuka is German. I find the fact that her name has little to no German aspect to it kind of ridiculous. Asuka, clearly a Japanese name, Soryu, again clearly Japanese. Langley being the closest thing with the origin being of English decent. Now we are not notified that her father is from America(a citation still needed on wiki) so I personally went ahead and disregarded that. I dont remember it being stated that Asuka, "although her nationality is American", is actually American (gonna listen for it next time i watch eva through should be soon). So basically from what ive gathered about Asuka is that she is 1/4 Japanese, and 3/4 German (Again im going on the basis that her fathers origins are unknown but her Blue eyes part leads me to think german or eastern eurpean as Blue eyes is a recessive DNA trait and there for both parents must hold this trait). Now i get that Japanese is a very fluid like language and does tend to have its own form of speaking completely different from German and that a traditional German Name wouldn't be too fitting in first place, such as being said all the time and just standing out in the middle of sentences so im okay with Asuka as her name, however i do believe that the rest of her name Langley and Soryu, more specifically soryu should be something of german origin. *note* feel free to mvoe this post if under wrong topic. Also i realize that i used some really lose ends to make a point but this is just sumthing that has bothered me for some time *note*

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sun Oct 10, 2010 8:59 am "Langley" is assumed to be American origin because it refers to an American WWII aircraft carrier, just as "Soryu" refers to a Japanese WWII aircraft carrier, and "Zeppelin" (Asuka's mother's German surname) refers to a German WWII aircraft carrier. Note the WWII naval theme of most of the surnames in the series (Katsuragi, Akagi, Aoba, Ibuki, Fuyutsuki, Ayanami, etc.)

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Sun Oct 10, 2010 11:22 am This is probably not going to be helpful but "Langley" happens to be a nicknamed for the CIA (Central Intelligence Agency) because it is located in Langley, Virginia.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Oct 10, 2010 3:43 pm Kyoko Zeppelin Soryu is 1/2 Japanese and 1/2 German. As we can see, she has a German and a Japanese surname, one from her father and one from her mother. Asuka Langley Soryu, whom we may be lead to believe is 1/4 Japanese, 1/4 German, and 1/2 American (though this cannot be confirmed) also takes her surnames from both parents. Langley from her father, and Soryu from her mother. Now, why did Soryu stick rather than Zeppelin? Well, the real reason is probably because the name 'Asuka Langley Soryu' came about in production much before 'Kyoko Zeppelin Soryu', and the only reason Zeppelin came about is because they needed to fit the German in there somewhere. However, for a canonical reason, the Japanese surname is the chosen surname because of the need to stick to one's own Japanese heritage; the Japanese are much more concerned with securing a blood line than the Germans are, especially when it comes to the child of mixed cultures. Had her name been Asuka Langely Zeppelin, any ties back to her Japanese heritage may have been cut, and it would have also sounded weird. Or I'm completely wrong, and the name is purely an aesthetic.

[]:             Quote Post by Eric Blair » Sun Oct 10, 2010 4:08 pm Sachi wrote: Or I'm completely wrong, and the name is purely an aesthetic.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Oct 10, 2010 4:16 pm Eric Blair wrote: Isn't the original Soryu Kyoko Zeppelin and Soyu Asuka Langley?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 02 » Sun Oct 10, 2010 5:01 pm I thought her mom was just a huge Led Zeppelin fan.

[]:             Quote Post by Eric Blair » Sun Oct 10, 2010 5:14 pm Sachi wrote: Surnames are still surnames, and in Japan the surnames to go in the front.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 02 » Sun Oct 10, 2010 5:23 pm Well, he did abandon her... Probably a Beatles fan.

[]:             Quote Post by Animejunkie » Sun Oct 10, 2010 7:43 pm Sachi wrote: . Now, why did Soryu stick rather than Zeppelin? Well, the real reason is probably because the name 'Asuka Langley Soryu' came about in production much before 'Kyoko Zeppelin Soryu', and the only reason Zeppelin came about is because they needed to fit the German in there somewhere. Or I'm completely wrong, and the name is purely an aesthetic.

[]:             Quote Post by Cody MacArthur Fett » Mon Oct 11, 2010 12:02 am Now see, here I really do have to ask, where is it ever confirmed that Asuka has two surnames? (Something which I've honestly never even heard of before now.) Near as I can figure she has a first name, middle name, and surname, and she just prefers to state her whole name when declaring herself. Granted, perhaps I'm just projecting since just about everybody on my mother's side of the family has a middle name, but it just seems much more reasonable to me to think that Langley and Zeppelin were close family members who were Kyoko and Asukas' namesakes rather then being their father's surnames.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Oct 11, 2010 12:53 am As we know, all the character surnames originate from warships. Langley comes the USS Langley, an American ship. Now why would an American name be used for a German character, born and raised, with Japanese heritage? All the other surnames are true to character nationality: Soryu is a Japanese aircraft carrier; the Graf Zeppelin was German. Langley is an obvious hint toward a third nationality, American. If Kyoko was 1/2 Japanese and 1/2 German, where could Asuka have gotten an American name from? Oh, her father. It's not uncommon for a child to have the surname of both parents, and if Kyoko did it becomes no surprise that Asuka did as well. Now, one could make the argument Kyoko is actually full Japanese, and the Zeppelin in her name is actually due to her marriage with Asuka's father, whose name may have been Zeppelin. It's also not uncommon for a woman to keep her original surname when married, and simply add her spouse's surname to the mix. However, this does not account for Asuka's two names; she's obviously not married. So this explanation is moot. For "Langley" and "Zeppelin" to be middle names of close family friends would be absolutely pointless and wouldn't give us as much character information that we do if we consider those names as surnames. With Kyoko Zeppelin Soryu we can obviously tell that she's a German/Japanese mix, which accounts for Asuka's German and Japanese side. "Asuka Langley Soryu" gives us insight on her third nationality, American, and of her American father, who we otherwise get no information about other than from his few lines in episode 22. Langley must be her father's surname, and he must obviously be American if we stick with the trend that the nationality of the warships the names originate from match the nationality of the characters with the surnames.

[]:             Quote Post by Cody MacArthur Fett » Mon Oct 11, 2010 6:29 am So it's all just assumption and speculation in the hopes that it gives insight tinto her character then? . . . Fine, whatever, it's not like it matters all that much anyway. Though it is worth noting that even if Langley and Zeppelin aren't surnames they can still give you insight into the characters' genetic history -- for instance, my real life middle name is German because it was my grandmother's maiden name and she was an immigrant from [East] Germany.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Mon Oct 11, 2010 7:23 am Cody MacArthur Fett wrote: So it's all just assumption and speculation in the hopes that it gives insight tinto her character then? . . .

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Oct 11, 2010 8:22 am Cody MacArthur Fett wrote: So it's all just assumption and speculation in the hopes that it gives insight tinto her character then? . . . Fine, whatever, it's not like it matters all that much anyway. Though it is worth noting that even if Langley and Zeppelin aren't surnames they can still give you insight into the characters' genetic history -- for instance, my real life middle name is German because it was my grandmother's maiden name and she was an immigrant from [East] Germany.

[]:             Quote Post by Eric Blair » Mon Oct 11, 2010 10:28 am Cody MacArthur Fett wrote: So it's all just assumption and speculation in the hopes that it gives insight tinto her character then?

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Mon Oct 11, 2010 11:41 am Eva 02 wrote: I thought her mom was just a huge Led Zeppelin fan.

[]:             Quote Post by Animejunkie » Mon Oct 11, 2010 2:21 pm Yea i basically got what i wanted out of it... i kinda have a strong sense of german pride being german and all... i just wanted to know where asukas german heritage was... the fact taht her mothers surname was zeppelin was good nough for me thats just something that never really sprung on me... And as to the American Part i like to think that just cause america is the way it is with its history of immigration just kinda leaves her fathers history blank, and allows you to make her father w/e nationality you want him to be. though her citizenship is considered to be american, im just gonna put this out there that because she grew up germany and went through german schools and what not i'd just say shes german not american... it doesnt really matter just kinda my way of thinking bout it... if u grew up and went to the schools and were raised based on those ideas thats what you are.

[]:             Quote Post by Eric Blair » Mon Oct 11, 2010 2:39 pm Animejunkie wrote: As to the American Part [...] just kinda leaves her father's history blank, and allows you to make her father w/e nationality you want him to be.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Oct 11, 2010 3:49 pm Animejunkie wrote: And as to the American Part i like to think that just cause america is the way it is with its history of immigration just kinda leaves her fathers history blank, and allows you to make her father w/e nationality you want him to be.
